page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-72 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4a server family. It features 72 dedicated vCPUs and 576.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This high-density memory allocation positions the instance for memory-intensive workloads that require substantial RAM per processing unit.

The instance configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage options. Additionally, the c4a-highmem-72 does not feature any integrated graphics processing units (GPUs) and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses, which requires users to configure external networking resources separately.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation combined with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides a dense resource profile for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets without the overhead of GPU hardware. This makes the instance suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise application servers, and memory-bound data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-72 is an x86_64 architecture server instance from the c4a family, configured with 72 dedicated vCPUs and 576.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, making it suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The instance does not include integrated GPUs or local storage, meaning all storage needs must be handled via network-attached volumes. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource profile is designed for memory-bound applications, such as large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, and heavy data processing tasks, offering a dense allocation of memory and dedicated compute resources without the added cost of specialized accelerator hardware. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance, while the lack of bundled local storage and GPUs allows for a streamlined, memory-focused hardware profile.
